page: Amazon Web Services i7ie.large is a storage-optimized instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8559C processor with x86_64 architecture, providing 2 dedicated vCPUs from 1 physical core with 2 threads. The CPU operates at a clock speed of 3.6 GHz and includes a 320 MB L3 cache. The instance is equipped with 16.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 6 GHz, yielding 8.0 GB of RAM per core. For local storage, it includes a bundled 1250 GB NVMe SSD. Baseline network bandwidth is rated at 2 Gbps.

Benchmark results highlight strong single-threaded performance alongside constrained multi-core capabilities. The instance achieves top-tier results in Geekbench single-core tests and strong performance in single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression. However, due to its 2-vCPU configuration, multi-core benchmarks such as stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core register in the lower percentiles. Memory bandwidth tests show weak to poor throughput for large uncached blocks, though Passmark memory scores remain average.

The i7ie.large offers a high ratio of local NVMe SSD storage and memory relative to its compute footprint, making it a resource-dense option for storage-heavy workloads that do not require massive parallel processing. The inclusion of advanced instruction sets like Intel AMX and AVX-512 provides acceleration for specific mathematical and cryptographic operations. This hardware profile is suited for lightweight database hosting, local caching layers, text processing, and single-threaded data compression tasks where high local storage capacity and fast single-core execution are prioritized over multi-threaded compute density.
description: Amazon Web Services i7ie.large is a storage-optimized instance utilizing the Nitro hypervisor and an Intel Xeon 8559C processor. It features 2 dedicated vCPUs (1 physical core), 16.0 GB of DDR5 memory, and a bundled 1250 GB NVMe SSD. Operating at 3.6 GHz with a 320 MB L3 cache, the CPU supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and Intel AMX. Benchmarks indicate top-tier single-core performance, particularly in text processing and single-threaded compression, though multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics are comparatively weak due to the low core count. With a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps and no GPU, this instance provides high storage density relative to its compute resources. It is best suited for lightweight databases, single-threaded data processing, and local caching workloads requiring substantial local NVMe storage.
og_description: Amazon Web Services i7ie.large is a storage-optimized instance featuring 2 vCPUs, 16 GB DDR5 RAM, and a 1250 GB NVMe SSD, delivering strong single-core performance for database and caching workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services i7ie.large is a storage-optimized instance with 2 vCPUs, 16 GB DDR5 RAM, and a 1250 GB NVMe SSD for database workloads.
tagline: A storage-optimized instance combining high-capacity local NVMe SSD storage with strong single-core performance and advanced Intel instruction sets.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8559C processor with 2 dedicated vCPUs and 3.6 GHz clock speed
16.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory (8.0 GB per core)
Bundled 1250 GB local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier single-core performance in Geekbench and compression tests
Supports AVX-512 and Intel AMX instruction sets
Optimized for lightweight databases, caching, and text processing
